ideas.one_liner: Occupancy-only correction based on tile size and SM resource limits.

sections.abandonment_condition: Occupancy-only variant does not improve MAPE on small-S configurations.
sections.falsification_path: Run the occupancy-only variant on held-out validation, especially small-S configurations; reject this candidate if it does not beat the accepted baseline on that slice.
hidden_assumptions: Tile size and SM resource limits are recoverable from the FA4 source and launch configuration.
Small-S prediction error is dominated by occupancy rather than memory hierarchy or precision throughput.
Occupancy can be modeled as a separable correction before adding bandwidth terms.
sections.hypothesis: An occupancy-only correction based on tile size and SM resource limits will reduce held-out MAPE for small-S FA4 configurations on B200.
sections.local_optimum_risk: Medium - this route is interpretable and cheap, but it may underfit precision and TMA/L2 effects.
sections.minimal_experiment: Implement only the occupancy factor; calibrate no memory or precision constants; compare held-out MAPE on small-S configurations against the baseline and C1 ablations.
sections.one_sentence_claim: Adding tile-size-dependent occupancy limits should reduce small-S runtime prediction error in the FA4 B200 roofline model.
sections.outside_family_alternative: C2 tests precision-only correction; C1 combines occupancy with TMA/L2 and precision terms.
sections.strongest_rejection_case: FA4 tile choices already achieve near-peak occupancy on B200, so the missing error comes from bandwidth, precision throughput, or scheduling.
sections.targeted_bottleneck: The FA4 roofline assumes full SM utilization.
sections.why_now: This is a direct test of the occupancy hypothesis and can be implemented without memory bandwidth calibration.

summary: Pre-idea draft for serious alternative C3.
title: Pre-Idea Draft: Occupancy-Only Correction
